contextual.title: Pathways Linking Reduced Sleep Duration and Quality to Obesity Risk
contextual.text:
Summary: This study is designed to study the pathways through which short sleep duration or poor sleep quality can lead to an increased risk of developing diabetes and obesity.
Inclusion criteria: inclusion criteria: 

 Normal weight 

 Healthy 

 Normal sleep times 

 
Exclusion criteria: : 

 Sleep disorders 

 Overweight 

 Diabetes 

 Other health conditions 

 Excessive caffeine and alcohol intake 

 Smoking

Obesity.definition: The amount of fat or lipid deposit at a site or an organ in the body, an indicator of body fat status.

Diabetes.definition: A heterogeneous group of disorders characterized by HYPERGLYCEMIA and GLUCOSE INTOLERANCE.
